title: Detecting lymphocyte aggregate-like niches in CRC VisiumHD data
text: metadata parquet files dataset can downloaded Zenodo GitHub. simplify DeconvolutionLabel1 column use input cell type labels. contains cell types 8-μ\mum binned data predicted deconvolution using single-cell RNA-seq reference. input data frame SCOPE contain following columns: CellID: cell id ImageID: sample id X: X coordinate Y: Y coordinate Celltype: cell type labels remove bins assigned “doublets” “rejected” deconvolution algorithm focus singlet bins.
code: library(arrow) # for loading parquet files  CRC_VisiumHD_metadata <- sapply(c("P1", "P2", "P5"), function(x) {   read_parquet(paste0("https://github.com/10XGenomics/HumanColonCancer_VisiumHD/",                       "raw/refs/heads/main/MetaData/", x, "CRC_Metadata.parquet"))   }, simplify = FALSE, USE.NAMES = TRUE) %>%   data.table::rbindlist(idcol = "ImageID") %>%   # Make cell ids unique   mutate(barcode = paste(barcode, ImageID, sep = "_")) %>%   # Simplifying cell type annotations   mutate(Celltype = case_when(     DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Mature B", "Memory B") ~ "B cell",       DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Proliferating Macrophages",                                 "Proliferating Immune II") ~ "Prolif_Immune",     DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Fibroblast", "Vascular Fibroblast",                                 "Proliferating Fibroblast") ~ "Fibroblast",      DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Enteric Glial", "Enterocyte", "Neuroendocrine") ~ "Neuronal",      str_detect(DeconvolutionLabel1, "Tumor") ~ "Tumor",     DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Epithelial", "Tuft", "Goblet") ~ "Epithelial",      DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Smooth Muscle", "SM Stress Response", "vSM") ~ "Smooth Muscle",       DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Unknown III (SM)", "Adipocyte") ~ "Other",     TRUE ~ DeconvolutionLabel1   )) CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in <- CRC_VisiumHD_metadata %>%   # Keeping only the singlet bins   filter(DeconvolutionClass == "singlet") %>%   select(CellID = barcode, ImageID, X = X, Y = Y, Celltype = Celltype)  # save(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, file = paste0("data/", dataset_name, "_dat_in.RData")) head(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in) # 810,330 bins #>                      CellID ImageID        X         Y Celltype #>                      <char>  <char>    <num>     <num>   <char> #> 1: s_008um_00000_00289-1_P1      P1 69.20113 -309.4240    Tumor #> 2: s_008um_00000_00290-1_P1      P1 69.44765 -309.4263    Tumor #> 3: s_008um_00000_00291-1_P1      P1 69.69418 -309.4286    Tumor #> 4: s_008um_00000_00292-1_P1      P1 69.94070 -309.4309    Tumor #> 5: s_008um_00000_00293-1_P1      P1 70.18723 -309.4332    Tumor #> 6: s_008um_00000_00294-1_P1      P1 70.43375 -309.4354    Tumor CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = celltype_colors,                       breaks = names(celltype_colors)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank())

title: Detecting lymphocyte aggregate-like niches in CRC VisiumHD data
text: Next, use k-nearest neighbors algorithm define spatial neighborhood compute neighborhood cell type composition matrix. default number nearest clusters kk 20, however can increase kk larger tissue slices. set kk=50. addition, enforce distance boundary nearest neighbors exclude cells falling outside radius. boundary initially defined 99th percentile distances focal cells nearest neighbors. falls outside predefined range, reset either minimum (min.radius) maximum threshold (max.radius). can examine distribution 99th percentile distance nearest neighbors select threshold parameters min.radius max.radius accordingly. 99th percentiles 2.466, 1.679, 1.939 P1, P2, P5 respectively, percentiles remain nearly identical across three samples. Therefore set max.radius 2 min.radius 1.  Cells retain fewer min.n.neighbors neighbors within distance boundary neighborhood cell-type proportions set NaN excluded subsequent niche clustering. set min.n.neighbors 10.
code: slide_list <- unique(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in$ImageID)  cell_neighbor_results_maxdist <- pbmcapply::pbmclapply(   slide_list, function(s) {     Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(       dat_slide=subset(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, ImageID == s),       knn_num = 50, cell_types_available = unique(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in$Celltype),       min.radius = 1,       # lower limit for the boundary       max.radius = 2,       # upper limit for the boundary       min.n.neighbors = 15, # minimum no. of neighbors       prob_list = seq(0, 1, 0.1))},   mc.cores = 3) # set mc.cores to 1 on Windows # save(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist,  #      file = paste0("data/", dataset_name, "_cell_neighbor_results.RData")) length(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist) #> [1] 3 sapply(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, "[[", "dist.quantiles") %>%   as.data.frame() %>%    rownames_to_column("perc") %>%   mutate(perc = as.numeric(str_remove(perc, "%"))) %>%   pivot_longer(2:ncol(.), names_to = "ImageID", values_to = "value") %>%   ggplot(aes(x = perc, y = value)) +   geom_point(aes(color = ImageID)) +   scale_color_discrete(labels = slide_list) +   scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) {paste0(x, "%")}) +   labs(y = "Distance to\nNearest Neighbors", x = "Percentile") +   theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) # Combine neighborhood proportion table from the three samples cell_neighbor_table_maxdist <- lapply(   cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, "[[", "prop.df") %>%   do.call("rbind", .) cells_to_exclude = apply(cell_neighbor_table_maxdist, 1, function(x) {any(is.na(x))}) %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   `colnames<-`("is_na") %>%   rownames_to_column("CellID") %>%   left_join(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in[, c("CellID", "X", "Y", "ImageID")], by = "CellID")  # Number of cells to exclude table(cells_to_exclude$is_na) #>  #>  FALSE   TRUE  #> 809156   1174  cells_to_exclude %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = is_na)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("brown2", "grey90"),                       breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE), name = "Cells To\nExclude") +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void() +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank())

what: 3. Cell type triad niche (CTN) detection using SCOPE
title: Detecting lymphocyte aggregate-like niches in CRC VisiumHD data
text: Next, apply SCOPE B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} triad using default configurations following parameters: min.prop: minimum neighborhood cell-type proportion threshold. Proportions value set 0 (default: 0.3). mgcv_df: dimension basis used thin-plate regression spline smoothing driver cell-type proportions (default: 15; see mgcv::s() details). set number clusters clusternum 3 separate B cell T cell zones. can see B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} CTNs closely aligns lymphocyte aggregates CRC samples, CTN1 resembles B cell zone CTN2 resembles surrounding T cell zone.
code: driver_celltypes <- c("B cell", "CD4 T cell", "CD8 T cell")  CTN_df <- run_SCOPE(   combination_table = as.data.frame(matrix(driver_celltypes, nrow = 1)),   cell_neighbor_table = cell_neighbor_table_maxdist,   dat_in = CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in,   save_results = FALSE,    clusternum = 3, min.prop = 0.3,   linear = FALSE, mgcv_df = 15, iter.max = 100) # save(CTN_df, file = paste0("data/", dataset_name, "_B_CD4T_CD8T.RData")) # Number of cells per CTN table(CTN_df$label) #>  #>       CTN1       CTN2 Unassigned  #>       9121       4242     795793 # CTN label with colored dots separating the cell types CTN_label <- color_CTN_names(   paste(driver_celltypes, collapse = "_"), celltype_colors)   # Visualizing B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell CTN CTN_df %>%   left_join(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = label)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("CTN1" = "#E69F00", "CTN2" = "#56B4E9",                                  "Unassigned" = "grey90"), name = NULL) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank())  # Show driver cell types only CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in %>%   mutate(Celltype = ifelse(Celltype %in% driver_celltypes, Celltype, "Other")) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = celltype_colors,                       breaks = names(celltype_colors)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = "Driver cell type triad") +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank()) CTN_df %>%   left_join(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   count(Celltype, label) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = n, y = label, fill = factor(Celltype, names(celltype_colors)))) +   geom_col(position = position_fill(reverse = TRUE)) +   scale_fill_manual(values = celltype_colors, name = NULL) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE, clip = "off") +   guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 3)) +   theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) +   labs(x = "Proportion of Cells", y = NULL) +   theme(plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5),         legend.key.size = unit(3, "mm"),         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom",         legend.location = "plot",         legend.justification = "right") sessionInfo() #> R version 4.4.1 title: Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data
text: rds file containing Seurat object dataset can downloaded Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) accesssion number GSE250346. downloaded GSE250346_Seurat_GSE250346_CORRECTED_SEE_RDS_README_082024.rds file saved <data_path>/. also use cells_partitioned_by_annotation.csv file GSE250346_HE_annotations.tar.gz contains annotations PF histopathological features (including TLSs). samples file need renamed following code original paper: input data frame SCOPE contain following columns: CellID: cell id ImageID: sample id X: X coordinate Y: Y coordinate Celltype: cell type labels four samples containing TLSs:
code: library(Seurat)  PF_Xenium_obj <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "GSE250346_Seurat_GSE250346_CORRECTED_SEE_RDS_README_082024.rds")) clinical_data <- distinct(PF_Xenium_obj@meta.data, sample, sample_affect)  # save(clinical_data, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_clinical_data.RData"))) load(paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_clinical_data.RData")) PF_Xenium_annot <- read.csv(paste0(data_path, "cells_partitioned_by_annotation.csv")) %>%   ## keeping only the relevant columns to reduce data size   select(CellID = cell_id, ImageID = sample, Annotation_Type, annotation_type_instance) %>%   mutate(patient = str_extract(ImageID, "(THD|TILD|VUHD|VUILD)[0-9]+")) %>%   left_join(clinical_data[, c("patient", "sample_affect")], by = "patient") %>%   ## Change sample name based on the code from the original paper (   ## Initial rename   mutate(ImageID_new = case_when(     sample_affect == "Less Affected" ~ paste0(patient, "LA"),     sample_affect == "More Affected"  ~ paste0(patient, "MA"),     TRUE ~ ImageID)) %>%   ## Fix additional names   mutate(ImageID_new = case_when(     ImageID == "TILD117MF" ~ "TILD117MA1",     ImageID == "TILD117MFB" ~ "TILD117MA2",     ImageID == "VUILD104LF" ~ "VUILD104MA1",     ImageID == "VUILD104MF" ~ "VUILD104MA2",     ImageID == "VUILD105LF" ~ "VUILD105MA1",     ImageID == "VUILD105MF" ~ "VUILD105MA2",     ImageID == "VUILD48LF" ~ "VUILD48LA1",     ImageID == "VUILD48MF" ~ "VUILD48LA2",     str_detect(ImageID_new, 'LF$') ~ str_replace(ImageID_new, "LF$", "LA"),     str_detect(ImageID_new, 'MF$') ~ str_replace(ImageID_new, "MF$", "MA"),     TRUE ~ ImageID_new)) %>%   mutate(CellID = paste(ImageID_new, CellID, sep = "_")) %>%   select(-ImageID) %>%   rename(ImageID = ImageID_new)  # save(PF_Xenium_annot, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_annotations.RData")) PF_Xenium_dat_in <- PF_Xenium_obj@meta.data %>%   select(CellID = full_cell_id, ImageID = sample,           X = x_centroid, Y = y_centroid, Celltype = final_CT) ## save(PF_Xenium_dat_in, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_dat_in.RData")) TLS_img_list <- unique(subset(PF_Xenium_annot, Annotation_Type == "TLS")$ImageID)  ## Images containing TLSs, colored by cell type PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   filter(ImageID %in% c(TLS_img_list)) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = PF_Xenium_celltype_palette,                       breaks = names(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3))) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1,         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom")  ## Images containing TLSs, colored by annotations PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_annot[, c("CellID", "Annotation_Type")], by = "CellID") %>%   filter(ImageID %in% c(TLS_img_list)) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Annotation_Type)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(breaks = names(annotation_palette), na.value = "grey90",                      values = annotation_palette, name = NULL) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3))) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1,         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom")

title: Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data
text: Next, use k-nearest neighbors algorithm define spatial neighborhood compute neighborhood cell type composition matrix. default number nearest clusters kk 20, however can increase kk larger tissues. Since dataset contains 3-5mm lung tissue cores, set k=50k=50. addition, enforce distance boundary nearest neighbors exclude cells falling outside radius. boundary initially defined 99th percentile distances focal cells nearest neighbors. falls outside predefined range, reset either minimum (min.radius) maximum threshold (max.radius). can examine distribution 99th percentile distance nearest neighbors select threshold parameters min.radius max.radius accordingly. set max.radius min.radius default values (500 100 respectively).  can see two outlier samples represent sparsely populated regions tissue.  cells retaining fewer min.n.neighbors neighbors within distance boundary, neighborhood cell-type proportions set NaN excluded subsequent niche clustering. set min.n.neighbors 10.
code:
slide_list <- unique(PF_Xenium_dat_in$ImageID)  cell_neighbor_results_maxdist <- pbmcapply::pbmclapply(   slide_list, function(s) {     Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(       dat_slide=subset(PF_Xenium_dat_in, ImageID == s),       knn_num = 50, cell_types_available = unique(PF_Xenium_dat_in$Celltype),       min.radius = 100,       # lower limit for the boundary       max.radius = 500,       # upper limit for the boundary       min.n.neighbors = 10,   # minimum no. of neighbors,       return.nn.ids = TRUE,   # Whether to return nearest neighbor Cell IDs       prob_list = seq(0, 1, 0.1))},   mc.cores = 4) # set mc.cores=1 on Windows machine  # Extract neighborhood cell count and ids and save it into a separate file cell_neighbor_counts_nnids <-  lapply(   cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, function(x) {x[c("count.df", "neighbor.cell.ids")]}) save(cell_neighbor_counts_nnids,      file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_cell_neighbor_counts_nnids.RData")) # Extract neighborhood cell type proportion and  # quantiles of distance to nearest neighbors cell_neighbor_props_dist.quantiles <-  lapply(   cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, function(x) {x[c("prop.df", "dist.quantiles")]}) save(cell_neighbor_props_dist.quantiles,       file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_cell_neighbor_props_dist.quantiles.RData"))  rm(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist) ## 99th percentile of the distance to nearest neighbors dist_99th <- sapply(cell_neighbor_props_dist.quantiles, "[[", "dist.quantiles") %>%   `colnames<-`(slide_list) %>%   as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("perc") %>%   mutate(perc = as.numeric(str_remove(perc, "%"))) %>%   pivot_longer(2:ncol(.), names_to = "ImageID", values_to = "value") %>%   filter(perc == 99)  ## Distribution of 99th percentile dist_99th %>%   ggplot(aes(x = value)) +   geom_histogram(color = "white", fill = "skyblue", bins = 20) +   geom_boxplot() +   geom_text(data = subset(dist_99th, value > 150),              aes(label = ImageID, y = 2),              hjust = 0, size = 3.5, angle = 90) +   labs(y = "99th percentile of Dist.
to Nearest Neighbors", x = NULL) +   theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, angle = 45)) img_outliers <- arrange(subset(dist_99th, value > 150), value)$ImageID PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   filter(ImageID %in% img_outliers) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = PF_Xenium_celltype_palette,                       breaks = names(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette),                      labels = rename_celltype, name = NULL) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 5)) +   facet_wrap(~ factor(ImageID, img_outliers), scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1,         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom") # Combine neighborhood proportion table cell_neighbor_table_maxdist <- lapply(   cell_neighbor_props_dist.quantiles, "[[", "prop.df") %>%   do.call("rbind", .) cells_to_exclude = apply(cell_neighbor_table_maxdist, 1, function(x) {any(is.na(x))}) %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   `colnames<-`("is_na") %>%   rownames_to_column("CellID") %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_dat_in[, c("CellID", "X", "Y", "ImageID")], by = "CellID")  # Number of cells to exclude table(cells_to_exclude$is_na) #>  #>   FALSE    TRUE  #> 1628906    1413  # Cells to be removed from the two sparse samples cells_to_exclude %>%   filter(ImageID %in% img_outliers) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = is_na, size = is_na)) +   geom_point(shape = 16) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("brown2", "grey90"),                       breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE), name = "Cells To
Exclude") +   scale_size_manual(values = c(0.1, 1)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1), size = "none") +   facet_wrap(~factor(ImageID, img_outliers), scales = "free") +   theme_void() +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         aspect.ratio = 1,         panel.background = element_blank()) # Cells to be removed from the samples containing TLSs cells_to_exclude %>%   filter(ImageID %in% TLS_img_list) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = is_na, size = is_na)) +   geom_point(shape = 16) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("brown2", "grey90"),                       breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE), name = "Cells To
Exclude") +   scale_size_manual(values = c(0.1, 0.5)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1), size = "none") +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void() +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         aspect.ratio = 1,         panel.background = element_blank())

what: 3. Cell type triad niche (CTN) detection using SCOPE
title: Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data
text: Next, apply SCOPE B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} triad using default configurations following parameters: min.prop: minimum neighborhood cell-type proportion threshold. Proportions value set 0 (default: 0.3). mgcv_df: dimension basis used thin-plate regression spline smoothing driver cell-type proportions (default: 15; see mgcv::s() details). set number clusters clusternum 3 separate B cell T cell zones. can see B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} CTNs closely aligns lymphocyte aggregates CRC samples, CTN1 resembles B cell zone CTN2 resembles surrounding T cell zone.   can compute evaluation metrics comparing CTN1 ground truth TLS annotations
code: driver_celltypes <- c("B cells", "CD4+ T-cells", "CD8+ T-cells")  CTN_df <- run_SCOPE(   combination_table = as.data.frame(matrix(driver_celltypes, nrow = 1)),   cell_neighbor_table = cell_neighbor_table_maxdist,   dat_in = PF_Xenium_dat_in,   save_results = FALSE,    clusternum = 3, min.prop = 0.3,   linear = FALSE, mgcv_df = 15, iter.max = 100) save(CTN_df, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_B_CD4T_CD8T.RData")) # Number of cells per CTN table(CTN_df$label) #>  #>       CTN1       CTN2 Unassigned  #>      42777      44617    1541512 # CTN label with colored dots separating the cell types CTN_label <- color_CTN_names(   paste(driver_celltypes, collapse = "_"), PF_Xenium_celltype_palette)   # Visualizing B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell CTN CTN_df %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   filter(ImageID %in% TLS_img_list) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = label)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("CTN1" = "#E69F00", "CTN2" = "#56B4E9",                                  "Unassigned" = "grey90"), name = NULL) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1)  # Show driver cell types only PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   filter(ImageID %in% TLS_img_list) %>%   filter(CellID %in% CTN_df$CellID) %>% # dropping cells with few neighbors   mutate(Celltype = ifelse(Celltype %in% driver_celltypes, as.character(Celltype), "Other")) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette, "Other" = "grey90")) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = "Driver cell type triad") +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank()) CTN_df %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   count(Celltype, label) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = n, y = label, fill = factor(Celltype, names(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette)))) +   geom_col(position = position_fill(reverse = TRUE)) +   scale_fill_manual(values = PF_Xenium_celltype_palette, name = NULL,                     labels = rename_celltype) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE, clip = "off") +   guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 4)) +   theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) +   labs(x = "Proportion of Cells", y = NULL) +   theme(plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5),         legend.key.size = unit(3, "mm"),         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom",         legend.location = "plot",         legend.justification = "right") library(aricode); library(caret) # for clustering evaluation  cluster_eval_metrics <- CTN_df %>%    mutate(label_binary = ifelse(label == "CTN1", "TLS", "Other")) %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_annot, by = "CellID") %>%   filter(ImageID %in% TLS_img_list) %>%   mutate(annot_binary = ifelse(Annotation_Type == "TLS", "TLS", "Other")) %>%   split(f = .$ImageID) %>%   lapply(function(df_sub) {     # ARI and NMI     ARI <- ARI(df_sub$label_binary, df_sub$annot_binary)     NMI <- NMI(df_sub$label_binary, df_sub$annot_binary)          # F-1 score     conf_mat <- confusionMatrix(       data = factor(df_sub$label_binary, c("TLS", "Other")),       reference = factor(df_sub$annot_binary, c("TLS", "Other")))     F1 <- conf_mat$byClass["F1"] #for multiclass classification problems          data.frame(ARI = ARI, NMI = NMI, F1 = F1)   }) %>%   rbindlist(idcol = "ImageID")  cluster_eval_metrics #>        ImageID       ARI       NMI        F1 title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
text: \(T\times T\) matrix containing CLQs every cell type pair, \(T\) number available cell types. rows matrix focal cell types columns neighboring cell types.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
text: colocalization pair cell types \(\) \(b\) tissue section \(l\) can evaluated CLQ metric $$CLQ_{b\rightarrow } = \left\{\begin{array}{ll} \frac{C_{b\rightarrow } / N_a}{N_b / (N - 1)} & N_a > 5 \mbox{} \ N_b > 5 \\ 0, & \text{otherwise} \end{array}\right.$$ \(C_{b\rightarrow }\) number cells cell type \(b\) within neighborhood cell type \(\), \(N_a\), \(N_b\), \(N\) number cells cell type \(\), \(b\) total number cells tissue section \(l\), respectively.

title: Computing observed colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ_observed
text: colocalization pair cell types \(\) \(b\) tissue section \(l\) can evaluated observed CLQ $$CLQ_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs} = \left\{\begin{array}{ll} 0, & N_a^{(l)} \leq 5 \mbox{ } \ N_b^{(l)} \leq 5 \\ \frac{C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs} / N_a^{(l)}}{N_b^{(l)} / (N^{(l)} - 1)}, & \text{otherwise} \end{array}\right.$$ \(C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs}\) number cells cell type \(b\) within neighborhood cell type \(\), \(N_a^{(l)}\), \(N_b^{(l)}\), \(N^{(l)}\) number cells cell type \(\), \(b\) total number cells tissue section \(l\), respectively.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — compute_CLQ_permutated
text: tissue section, cell type labels randomly shuffled iter.num (default: 500) times generate permutated CLQ values $$CLQ_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k} = \left\{\begin{array}{ll} 0, & N_a^{(l)} \leq 5 \mbox{ } \ N_b^{(l)} \leq 5 \\ \frac{C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k} / N_a^{(l)}}{N_b^{(l)} / (N^{(l)} - 1)}, & \text{otherwise} \end{array}\right.$$ \(C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k}\) number cells cell type \(b\) within neighborhood cell type \(\) iteration \(k, k=\)1,2,...,iter.num, \(N_a^{(l)}\), \(N_b^{(l)}\), \(N^{(l)}\) number cells cell type \(\), \(b\) total number cells tissue section \(l\), respectively.

Can set 1 Windows. rename_CTN Whether rename niches based proportion driver cell types. cluster highest proportion named CTN clusternum = 2 CTN1 otherwise. cluster lowest driver cell type proportion renamed Unassigned. clusters named CTN2, ... accordingly clusternum > 2. save_results Whether save niche screening results output directory.
